question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DomainUtils.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: convertToNanosMax(long second, long nanos)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a token transaction with a seconds value large enough to trigger positive overflow in convertToNanosMax(), causing the returned Long.MAX_VALUE to be stored as the token's freeze-related expiry timestamp, permanently locking associated token balances?
